---
title: "DU07: DuckDB Technical Specifications"
subtitle: "Complete Technical Specifications for DuckDB"
author: "MAMBA Framework"
date: "2025-08-27"
categories: [database, duckdb, specifications]
---

# DuckDB Technical Specifications

## Overview

DuckDB is an in-process SQL OLAP database management system designed for analytical query workloads. It provides excellent performance for analytical queries while maintaining a simple deployment model with zero external dependencies.

## Supported Data Types

### General-Purpose Data Types

| Type | Aliases | Description | Size/Range |
|------|---------|-------------|------------|
| **BIGINT** | INT8, LONG | Signed eight-byte integer | -2^63 to 2^63-1 |
| **INTEGER** | INT4, INT, SIGNED | Signed four-byte integer | -2^31 to 2^31-1 |
| **SMALLINT** | INT2, SHORT | Signed two-byte integer | -2^15 to 2^15-1 |
| **TINYINT** | INT1 | Signed one-byte integer | -2^7 to 2^7-1 |
| **HUGEINT** | - | Signed sixteen-byte integer | -2^127 to 2^127-1 |
| **UBIGINT** | - | Unsigned eight-byte integer | 0 to 2^64-1 |
| **UINTEGER** | - | Unsigned four-byte integer | 0 to 2^32-1 |
| **USMALLINT** | - | Unsigned two-byte integer | 0 to 2^16-1 |
| **UTINYINT** | - | Unsigned one-byte integer | 0 to 2^8-1 |
| **UHUGEINT** | - | Unsigned sixteen-byte integer | 0 to 2^128-1 |
| **DOUBLE** | FLOAT8 | Double precision floating-point | 8 bytes |
| **FLOAT** | FLOAT4, REAL | Single precision floating-point | 4 bytes |
| **DECIMAL(p,s)** | NUMERIC(p,s) | Fixed-precision decimal | Default: p=18, s=3 |
| **VARCHAR** | CHAR, BPCHAR, TEXT, STRING | Variable-length character string | No limit |
| **BOOLEAN** | BOOL, LOGICAL | Logical boolean | true/false/NULL |
| **DATE** | - | Calendar date | year, month, day |
| **TIME** | - | Time of day (no timezone) | HH:MM:SS.ffffff |
| **TIMESTAMP** | DATETIME | Date and time combination | Microsecond precision |
| **TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE** | TIMESTAMPTZ | Timestamp with timezone | Current timezone |
| **INTERVAL** | - | Date/time delta | Flexible duration |
| **BLOB** | BYTEA, BINARY, VARBINARY | Variable-length binary data | No limit |
| **BIT** | BITSTRING | String of 1s and 0s | Variable length |
| **UUID** | - | UUID data type | 128-bit |
| **JSON** | - | JSON object (via extension) | Requires json extension |

### Nested/Composite Data Types

DuckDB supports five nested data types for complex data structures:

| Type | Description | Key Characteristics |
|------|-------------|-------------------|
| **ARRAY** | Fixed-length ordered sequence | All elements same type, fixed size |
| **LIST** | Variable-length ordered sequence | All elements same type, variable size |
| **MAP** | Key-value pairs | Keys and values can be different types |
| **STRUCT** | Named fields | Each field can be different type |
| **UNION** | Alternative types | Stores one of multiple types |

#### Important Notes on Nested Types:
- **LIST** columns can contain different lengths per row but must have same element type
- **STRUCT** requires all rows to have same fields
- **MAP** allows different keys per row
- **UNION** can store different member types per row
- Nested types can be arbitrarily nested to any depth

## Database Limitations

### Size Limits

| Component | Limit | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Database file size | No hard limit | Limited by disk space |
| Table size | No hard limit | Limited by available memory/disk |
| Row size | No specific limit | Practical limit based on memory |
| Column count | 1000+ columns | Soft limit, can be higher |
| JSON object size | 16,777,216 bytes | Default, configurable |
| String length | No hard limit | Limited by available memory |

### Performance Considerations

1. **Memory Usage**: DuckDB can operate entirely in memory or spill to disk
2. **Parallelization**: Automatic multi-threading for query execution
3. **Vectorized Execution**: Processes data in chunks for CPU efficiency
4. **Column Storage**: Columnar storage format optimized for analytics

## File Format Support

### Native Reading Support
- **CSV**: Full support with auto-detection
- **Parquet**: Native high-performance reader
- **JSON/NDJSON**: Flexible schema detection
- **Arrow**: Zero-copy data exchange

### Reading Parameters

#### JSON Reading Parameters
- `auto_detect`: Auto-detect schema (default: true)
- `columns`: Explicitly specify column types
- `maximum_depth`: Nesting depth for type detection (default: -1)
- `sample_size`: Objects to sample for auto-detection (default: 20480)
- `format`: array, newline_delimited, unstructured, auto
- `records`: Parse as records (default: auto)
- `compression`: none, gzip, zstd, auto_detect
- `maximum_object_size`: Max JSON object size in bytes

## Configuration Options

### Key Configuration Settings

```sql
-- Memory limit
SET memory_limit='10GB';

-- Thread count
SET threads=4;

-- Temp directory
SET temp_directory='/path/to/temp';

-- Preserve insertion order
SET preserve_insertion_order=true;
```

## Extension System

DuckDB supports extensions for additional functionality:

```sql
-- Install and load JSON extension
INSTALL json;
LOAD json;

-- Other useful extensions
INSTALL httpfs;    -- Read from HTTP/S3
INSTALL postgres;  -- PostgreSQL compatibility
INSTALL sqlite;    -- SQLite compatibility
```

## Type Conversion Rules

### Implicit Casting
DuckDB performs implicit casting in many scenarios:
- Numeric types cast to wider types automatically
- Strings cast to numbers when valid
- Timestamps cast to dates by truncation

### Explicit Casting
Use `::` or `CAST()` for explicit conversion:
```sql
SELECT '42'::INTEGER;
SELECT CAST('2024-01-01' AS DATE);
SELECT '["a","b","c"]'::JSON;
```

## Best Practices

1. **Use Appropriate Types**: Choose the narrowest type that fits your data
2. **Leverage LIST for Arrays**: More flexible than PostgreSQL arrays
3. **JSON for Semi-Structured**: Use JSON type for flexible schemas
4. **Partition Large Tables**: Use Hive-style partitioning for large datasets
5. **Compression**: Enable compression for Parquet files
6. **Batch Operations**: Use batch inserts for better performance

## Common Gotchas

1. **LIST Column Storage**: Cannot be directly written to some formats
2. **JSON Comparison**: Physical vs logical comparison differences
3. **NULL Handling**: Three-valued logic in comparisons
4. **Time Zones**: TIMESTAMP vs TIMESTAMPTZ behavior
5. **Memory Limits**: Default memory limit may need adjustment

## References

- [DuckDB Official Documentation](https://duckdb.org/docs/)
- [DuckDB GitHub Repository](https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb)
- [DuckDB Performance Guide](https://duckdb.org/docs/guides/performance/)
